hook.text: Digg was Reddit before Reddit mattered. Then a single disastrous redesign killed it in one week.

buildup.text: Digg was the internet's front page — users voted stories up or down. At its peak, it drove more traffic than Google News.

discovery.text: Digg v4 launched in 2010 and removed user voting power, replacing it with publisher-curated content and auto-submissions.

twist.text: The community felt betrayed. Power users organized an exodus to Reddit. The top story on Digg's front page: 'How to use Reddit.'

climax.text: Digg lost 26% of its traffic in one month. It eventually sold for just $500,000 — down from a rejected $200 million offer.

punchline.text: Take away what users love, and they'll find it somewhere else.

quiz.question: What caused Digg's collapse?
options: A redesign that removed user voting power, causing a mass exodus to Reddit
Digg's servers couldn't handle the traffic
